programmes.show_name: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programmes.show_description: Alison bonds with a giddy bulldog cross puppy who has no concept of personal space and an unhealthy obsession with her gloves. A lively frenchie needs to rest to repair a leg injury and a Bernese mountain dog tries to overcome her fear of just about everything

programmes.show_name: For the Love of Dogs with Alison Hammond
programmes.show_description: Alison meets the most unlikely stray to be wandering the London streets in the form of a rare Romanian mioritic shepherd who has a fear of stairs and doorways. She also helps a tiny Bichon control its bark, and there is organised chaos when a van with seven Belgian shepherd cross puppies arrives from the RSPCA
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The chef is on the road in his bespoke vintage food truck in Andalucia. Wine chemist Joaquin explains the patience needed to produce sherry vinegar in the famed sherry triangle, before putting Tom to the test alongside blender Ramon to see if he can produce a blend good enough to match their best vintage. Tom's journey then takes him to Cadiz, where avocados have become big business. There local producer Rafa shows him the work being done to help grow this thirsty plant

Beginning in Cuenca with third-generation garlic producer Elena - who puts Tom to work on a high-tech tractor and gets him to taste the intensity of garlic fresh from the ground - his journey then takes him to Murcia, the fruit basket of Spain, to learn from producer Cristina what makes this region a produce powerhouse
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Good Morning Britain's Charlotte Hawkins and Sean Fletcher go head-to-head with Cold Feet actors Fay Ripley and John Thomson for charity

Alan Titchmarsh, Frances Tophill and Danny Clarke are in Leicester to create a garden dedicated to the celebration of all things textural for a hard-working support worker, who puts in long hours looking after young single mothers. Meanwhile, David Domoney creates a prairie inspired split level garden, full of swaying grasses and calming planting for a police researcher whose busy job means she struggles to find time to rest and relax

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are Tessa Peake-Jones, Adam Garcia and Phil Daniels, while garden designer Chris Hull has the latest on multi-stem trees. Ian Burrell gives a masterclass in no-frills rum, and there is a visit to a farm that is helping adults with disabilities and autism through nature
A round-up of the latest headlines

James celebrates the Easter weekend with actress Patsy Kensit, who talks about appearing in the new series of Pilgrimage, and he is slo joined by chefs Ryan and Liam Simpson-Trotman and Lisa Goodwin-Allen, and forager Alysia Vasey

Raymond visits Hillsborough Castle in Co Down, the official royal residence in Northern Ireland, where he spends time soaking up the tranquillity of the gardens. During a visit to the garden shop and a chat with gardener Edele, Raymond sees what visitors have the chance to take home, while cafe chef Jamie prepares a fresh dish filled with produce from the gardens for Raymond

James explores Birmingham with acclaimed food critic, broadcaster, columnist and author Grace Dent. While taking a trip down the canal on a narrow boat, he cooks up a chicken balti, then he makes a steamed sponge pudding with rhubarb compote and custard.
Round-up of the latest headlines

Oli Bell presents live coverage of a meeting headlined by the Queen's Cup from Musselburgh, plus the finals of the Challenger Series from Haydock Park. With analysis from Leonna Mayor and Adele Mulrennan, reporting by Matt Chapman and Natalie Green, and commentary from Mark Johnson

Bradley Walsh presents as Toby Anstis, Paula Radcliffe, Rav Wilding and Vanessa Feltz pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
Round-up of the latest headlines

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Aisling Bea, Chris Packham and Kate Lawler all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of today's performers be just what the pair are looking for?

Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in fake show The Great Kitchen Cook Off, hosted by MasterChef: The Professionals' Monica Galetti, who is in on the whole thing. Former England footballer Jill Scott is the guest saboteur

Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?
The host is joined by Slow Horses star Kristin Scott Thomas and comedian Katherine Ryan, while music comes courtesy of singer-songwriter Sienna Spiro
Round-up of the latest headlines
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

James celebrates the Easter weekend with actress Patsy Kensit, who talks about appearing in the new series of Pilgrimage, and he is slo joined by chefs Ryan and Liam Simpson-Trotman and Lisa Goodwin-Allen, and forager Alysia Vasey

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar in the kitchen by musician Tanita Tikaram for cooking, chat and craft. After Japanese salmon miso and natto for breakfast, Jimmy and Shivi set about rustling up a peanut stew before all three try making homemade treats for pets. Shivi gets her waders on for an encounter with beavers at a city rewilding project, Jimmy visits a family doughnut kitchen and store in Florida, and there's a new twist on a classic margarita cocktail
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm for a special Easter Sunday show are actress Sheila Hancock and West End star Julian Ovenden, while florist Simon Lycett has the best in Easter bouquets. Plus, a couple who have set up a hedgehog hospital in their back garden, and things get very chocolatey in the Best of British feature with drinks expert Tom Surgey

It's scanning time on the farm and the family are about to find out whether their three male tups have earned their keep and got the ewes in lamb, with all four children are on hand to help. Liz and Kelvin turn to their socials to drum up interest in their first farmers market, Marnie makes a decision on what prime cuts she wants to sell, and Milo helps his dad load up the Jacob sheep that are heading for the butcher

Alan Titchmarsh creates an accessible garden in Derbyshire for Jack Parrott, a D-Day veteran approaching his 100th birthday. The team seeks out advice on clever new ways to create raised beds and patio-size greenhouses to find ways of allowing Jack to once again indulge in his life-long love of gardening
Round-up of the latest headlines

Ant and Dec host as the auditions continue, with more acts taking to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and KSI. With the presenters yet to push their Golden Buzzer, might one of today's performers be just what the pair are looking for?

Ben Shephard asks the questions as Roisin Conaty, Chris Bavin and Jordan Banjo answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

All that stands between Callum from Nuneaton, Miriam from Lancashire, Hyder from Glasgow and Linda from London and that money is the small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains. Bradley Walsh hosts
Round-up of the latest headlines
Marlon is devastated, while Cain is rocked and Kerry is riddled with guilt

Ben forces Maggie to reveal that Alan was not his father - but can she keep Jim McDonald a secret? Meanwhile, Debbie encounters Carl upon her release from custody, and Sam reluctantly accepts Will's apology

As Roy and Branson take steps to verify Cassian's intel about Sandy's murder, the body of student Charlie Downing is found in a sea lock, and Grace must find out who wanted this young woman dead. As the team investigates the campus, it becomes clear that the university harbours secrets that could be connected to Charlie's death. Grace soon has his sights set on a killer who will stop at nothing to prevent one particular truth from getting out
Round-up of the latest headlines
